preamble: The transformation of [æ] to [u] is a significant shift involving both backing and extreme raising, typically found in contexts with strong labial or velar influence.

examples.note: Historical shifts in specific lexical items or morphological processes involving rounding.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
